name: textualdon

metadata.name: textualdon
metadata.summary: A Mastodon client built with the Textual framework

documentation:
# TextualDon
A Mastodon client built with the Textual framework for Python.

### Current version: 0.1.0

![Demo Image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/edward-jazzhands/textualdon/refs/heads/master/graphic1.png)

TextualDon aims to be a full-featured, graphical Mastodon client that lives inside of your terminal.

It offers a new take on 'social media in the terminal' compared to other apps. 
TextualDon leverages the full power of Textual to provide an almost website-like 
experience in a keyboard-centric TUI format.

### Features:

- For keyboard lovers: Designed for 100% keyboard control (Or just mouse, or a mix).
- Cross-platform: Works across Windows, Mac, and Linux.
- Auto login and load specific pages on launch
- Support for images (And option to disable downloading them entirely).
- Looks good in most default Textual themes
- Many more to come

This app is in early Alpha stages. Many essential features are not built yet.   
This is only public to get feedback from advanced users. If you choose to use it, 
please keep in mind the app is not finished.

modules.name: textualdon.imageviewer
documentation:
NOTE: This module is a modified version of the ImageViewer class from the textual_imageview package.
See LICENSES file for more information.

classes.name: textualdon.imageviewer.ImageViewer

methods.name: textualdon.imageviewer.ImageViewer.__init__
documentation:
A widget that displays an image and allows zooming and panning.

Args:
    image (Image.Image): The image to display.
    min_zoom (int, optional): The minimum zoom level. Defaults to 10.
    max_zoom (int, optional): The maximum zoom level. Defaults to 1.
    nested (bool, optional): Whether the ImageViewer will be a child inside another Widget. Defaults to False.
    id: The ID of the widget in the DOM.

Setting `nested` to True will make the ImageViewer only capture the mouse scroll up/down when its parent has focus.
This is useful when it's a child of another Widget, or you just want to control when it's allowed to zoom.

Note: passing through *args and **kwargs to Widget class is blocked except for the `id` parameter.

methods.name: textualdon.imageviewer.ImageViewer.on_mouse_scroll_down
documentation: scroll down to zoom out

methods.name: textualdon.imageviewer.ImageViewer.on_mouse_scroll_up
documentation: scroll up to zoom in
NOTE: This class is a modified version of the ImageViewer class from the textual_imageview package.

modules.name: textualdon.sql
documentation: Standard library imports

classes.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.__init__
documentation:
Create a new SQLite database wrapper.    
Must pass in `app_name`: the name of the package that is using this widget.   
This same name will be used to create a directory in the user's data directory to store the database file.

`del_on_start` will delete the existing database file and create a new one (for dev mode).

Args:
    app_name: This must be the name of the package that is using this widget.
        This MUST be the same name as your program / main package.
    data_dir: Directory on user's computer where the folder will be created.
    sql_script: The name of the SQL script file to use for setting up the database tables and schema.   
        Path is relative to the main package directory.
    db_filename: The name the database file will use. If None, name will be `<app_name>.db`.
    del_on_start: Whether to delete the database file on start. Defaults to False.
    name: The name of the widget.
    id: The ID of the widget in the DOM.
    classes: The CSS classes for the widget.

EXAMPLE:
```
db = SQLite("my_package", "create_tables.sql")
```

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite._cursor
documentation: Used internally by class

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.execute_script
documentation:
Execute a SQL script on the database.

EXAMPLE:
```
script = "create_table.sql"
db.execute_script(script)
```

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.insert_one
documentation:
Insert a single row into the database.   
Using the auto_commit parameter, you can stage multiple inserts before committing them all at once.
Simply let it go back to the default value of True to commit all inserts.

EXAMPLE:
```    
table = "users"
columns = ["name", "age", "email"]
values = ["Alice", 30, "alice@example.com"]
db.insert_one(table, columns, values)
```
Raw SQL:   
`INSERT INTO {table} ({', '.join(columns)}) VALUES (?, ?, ?);`

Args:
    table (str): The name of the table to insert the row into.
    columns (list[str]): The list of column names.
    values (Sequence[Any]): List or tuple of values to insert.
    auto_commit (bool, optional): Whether to commit the transaction automatically. Defaults to True.

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.delete_one
documentation:
Delete a row from a database table.

EXAMPLE:
```
db.delete_one('employees', 'id', 3)
```
Raw SQL:   
`DELETE FROM {table_name} WHERE {column_name} = ?;`

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.update_column
documentation:
Update a column in a database table.

EXAMPLE:
```
db.update_column('employees', 'salary', 75000, 'id', 3)
``` 
Raw SQL:   
`UPDATE {table_name} SET {column_name} = ? WHERE {condition_column} = ?; `

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.fetchall
documentation:
This method runs a SQL query and retrieves all rows that match the query criteria.

EXAMPLE:
```
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?"
params = ("Alice",)   # Note the comma, (must be sequence)
rows = db.fetchall(query, params)
``` 
Or use the shortcut:
```
row = db.fetchone("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?", ("Alice",))
```
Args:
    query (str): The SQL query to run.
    params (Sequence, optional): The query parameters. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    list[tuple]: A list of rows that match the query criteria.

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.fetchone
documentation:
This method is similar to fetchall, but it only retrieves a single row
from the database, even if multiple rows meet the query criteria.

EXAMPLE:
```
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?"
params = ("Alice",)   # Note the comma, (must be sequence)
row = db.fetchone(query, params)
``` 
Or use the shortcut:
```
row = db.fetchone("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?", ("Alice",))
```

Args:
    query (str): The SQL query to run.
    params (Sequence, optional): The query parameters. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    tuple: A single row that matches the query criteria.

methods.name: textualdon.sql.SQLite.close
documentation: Close the database connection.

A simple SQLite database wrapper for TextualDon.   
Cannot attach child widgets (blocked).

modules.name: textualdon.simplebutton
documentation: Standard library imports

classes.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton

methods.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.__init__
documentation:
Custom constructor for SimpleButton. Adds 'justify', 'overflow', and 'no_wrap' arguments for
the Rich Text class. args and kwargs are passed to the Static constructor.

Args:
    renderable: A Rich renderable, or string containing console markup.
    justify (str, optional): Justify method: "left", "center", "full", "right". Defaults to None.
    overflow (str, optional): Overflow method: "crop", "fold", "ellipsis". Defaults to None.
    no_wrap (bool, optional): Disable text wrapping, or None for default. Defaults to None.
    index (int, optional): Allows setting index for the button. This is useful if the button
        is part of a list of buttons. Defaults to None.
    expand: Expand content if required to fill container.
    shrink: Shrink content if required to fill container.
    markup: True if markup should be parsed and rendered.
    name: Name of widget.
    id: ID of Widget.
    classes: Space separated list of class names.
    disabled: Whether the static is disabled or not.
## END MESSAGES ###
##  SimpeButton MAIN  ###

methods.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.render
documentation: Pass the options from the constructor into the Rich Text class.

methods.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.watch_mouse_hover
documentation:
OVERRIDE: Update from CSS if mouse over state changes.
Textual addition: posts HoverEnter / HoverLeave messages.

methods.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.on_click
documentation:
Called when the button is clicked. Posts a message 'Pressed'.
Use the message handler to handle the event:   
```
@on(SimpleButton.Pressed)   
def on_button_pressed(self, message: SimpleButton.Pressed) -> None:   
    print("Button was pressed!")
```

inner_classes.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.Pressed

methods.name: textualdon.simplebutton.SimpleButton.Pressed.control
documentation:
This is required to be able to use the 'selector' property
when using the message handler.

Event sent when a `SimpleButton` is pressed.   

Can be handled using `on_simple_button_pressed` (in a subclass of
[`Button`][textual.widgets.Button] or in a parent widget in the DOM.)   
OR   
by using @on(SimpleButton.Pressed)
## MESSAGES ###

A simple button widget. Note that this does not inherit from Button, but from Static.

This is designed to copy the basic functionality of the Textual button, but in a
more minimalistic way that changes some of the core functionality and appearance.

It can be any size (down to 1x1). It copies the `Pressed` event from normal buttons.
It also renders itself using the rich Text class, so you can set
the overflow and no_wrap properties.

By default, no_wrap is `True` and overflow is `crop`. This meas the button
text will not wrap and will be cropped if it is too long. You can change this by using
the 'no_wrap' and 'overflow' arguments in the constructor.

It also contains the `HoverEnter` and `HoverLeave` events, which are triggered when the
mouse enters or leaves the button. These provide convenient messages for hovering over the button,
so that the hover event can be handled by the parent widget.

EXAMPLE USAGE:
- yield button:
```
yield SimpleButton("your_button_text", id="sample_id")
```
- In some cases you'll want word wrap enabled, without overflow:
```
yield SimpleButton("your_button_text", no_wrap=None, overflow=None)
```
- message handler:
```
@on(SimpleButton.Pressed)   
def on_button_pressed(self, message: SimpleButton.Pressed) -> None:   
    print("Button was pressed!")
```

modules.name: textualdon.oauth
documentation: Standard library imports

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.trigger_login
documentation: This is triggered by the SavedUsersManager widget when a user is selected.

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.oauth_flow
documentation: @on(OAuthInput.Submitted)
async def submit_input(self, event: OAuthInput.Submitted) -> None:
self.log.debug("Trigger function: submit_input in oauth.py")
await self.oauth_flow()
##~ Oauth Flow ~###

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.login_stage1
documentation:
Stage 1: Registers the app with the Mastodon instance.   
This should only be run once and it will save the app profile to a file.

Returns:
    tuple[str, str]: The client ID and client secret.
Stages 1 and 2 are both in the group 'login', and exclusive=True.
it probably doesn't matter much but it might help prevent it being
run twice at the same time for some reason.

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.login_stage2
documentation:
Stage 2: Submits app credentials to the Mastodon instance.   
Requests an auth URL from the instance, opens the browser.  

Returns:
    tuple[Mastodon, str]: The Mastodon object and the auth url.

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.login_stage4
documentation:
Stage 4: Logs in with the auth code received from the callback server.

Returns:
    str: The access token.

methods.name: textualdon.oauth.OAuthWidget.login_stage5
documentation:
Stage 5: Updates the UI and Database.

THIS FUCTION IS CALLED BY:
- OauthWidget.oauth_flow
- OauthWidget.trigger_login

Passing in an access token means its the first time logging in with that account.   

Returns:
    str: The display name of the logged in user.

methods.name: textualdon.toot.TootOptionHandler.handle_toot_action
documentation:
Every Mastodon action passed into this method must take either a toot_id 
or a user_id as its first argument. This is determined by the
`on_user` parameter. If `on_user` is True, the action is on the user (toot author).
If `on_user` is False, the action is on the status (the toot itself).

'key' is the entry in the returned JSON dict that will be checked to see if the action
was successful. If the action is successful, the value of the key should be different
from the ingoing status. If the action is unsuccessful, the value of the key should be the same.

inner_classes.name: textualdon.toot.TootOptionHandler.StatusKey

class_variables.name: textualdon.toot.TootOptionHandler.StatusKey.MUTED
documentation: muted is for the status / conversation. 'mute this conversation'

class_variables.name: textualdon.toot.TootOptionHandler.StatusKey.MUTING
documentation: muting is for the user. 'mute this user'
These are the keys that are checked in the returned JSON dict to see if the action was successful.

documentation:
This is used by the Mastodon proxy class in proxy.py
Would have preferred to put it in that class but it did not work for strange reasons.

methods.name: textualdon.__main__.TextualDon.capture_exceptions
documentation:
Context manager that captures and forwards exceptions to the async error handler. 


This is here to allow the custom ErrorHandler class to be async. Regular sync functions
or exceptions can use this context manager to capture exceptions (replacing the `try/except` block).
```
with self.capture_exceptions():
    # dangerous code here
```

methods.name: textualdon.__main__.TextualDon.handle_exception
documentation:
Exceptions caught with capture_exceptions (above) are sent here for transfer to
async error handler class.

methods.name: textualdon.__main__.TextualDon.intro_screens_callback
documentation:
Function to manage the two intro screens. If both are active, the first callback will         focus the second screen instead of the login widget. 

The first argument is required to be here because self.dismiss in the screen class         always sends an argument. If nothing is provided it sends `None`.

methods.name: textualdon.__main__.TextualDon.focus_login
documentation: Triggered by intro_screens_callback above, or on_mount if there's no intro popup.

methods.name: textualdon.__main__.TextualDon.login_complete
documentation:
Event sent by OauthWidget.login_stage5 when it completes.
Refreshes page if auto-load is enabled.
##~ EVENT HANDLERS ~###

- toot.TextAreaEdit.focus (overridden method)
- tootbox.TextAreaReply.focus (overridden method)

and page changing has to be handled in the main app.
